data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-06f820f4">
    <title>install/update: add --global flag and dual-location install honoring convention vs in-tree mode</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
`ddx install` and `ddx update` only ever target the per-project plugin tree. There is no way to install a skill/persona/plugin once per machine, so operators must repeat installs in every project. Three install destinations must be supported: global (XDG), project in-tree (`<project>/.ddx`), and project convention (XDG projects/...).

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go:36 `RunE: f.runInstall` and its flag set (install.go:38-39) have no `--global` flag; install always targets the project under `.ddx/plugins/<name>/` (install.go:27-28).
- cli/cmd/update.go:57 `runUpdate` and update.go:194 `performUpdate` operate only on the working dir's project tree; update.go:112 `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets(workingDir)` is project-scoped.
- Both route through ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and never reference the global layer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` bool flag to `ddx install` (cli/cmd/install.go) and `ddx update` (cli/cmd/update.go).
2. install --global: write content to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` (via `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` from the resolver bead) and create symlinks/copies into agent-tier paths `~/.claude/skills/<name>` and `~/.agents/skills/<name>` (copy on platforms lacking symlink support).
3. install (no --global): keep writing to `ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/` and link into `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` AND `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`. In in-tree mode the target is `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>`; in convention mode it is `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>` and the project agent-tier links point into that XDG path.
4. update --global updates only the global plugin tree; update (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode (in-tree vs convention) is active, via `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets`.

NON-SCOPE
- The plugin resolver/precedence (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution) — consume `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` from it.
- `ddx init --global` first-time setup (sibling bead: init topology).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Removing per-project install; both modes coexist.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution); sibling registry-resolver bead (provides `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` and layer semantics).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `ddx install <name> --global --silent` installs to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` and creates a symlink or copy under `~/.claude/skills/<name>` and `~/.agents/skills/<name>` — TestInstallGlobal_WritesXDGAndAgentTierLinks.
2. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` and creates project-tier links under `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>` — TestInstallProject_InTreeLinks.
3. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/` and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins path — TestInstallProject_ConventionLinks.
4. `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and leaves the project tree untouched — TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject.
5. `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active — TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
Un-parked 2026-06-02: wedged on the progress_watchdog false-kill (empty route) now fixed by D1 (ddx-6190edc6, merged c782ba42 — records the fizeau-resolved route in liveness). Retrying with the corrected watchdog.
    </notes>
    <labels>area:plugin, area:cli, kind:feature, phase:3, initiative:zero-footprint, principle:skills-personas</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-937de9fc" base-rev="c58959b17972e6a01f2f41edbb168b6beeee27ae" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260602T182830-d5fb198b"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

candidates.reason: model "anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5" has no catalog power metadata

data.output:
The commit landed cleanly (`2b3ff49f0`) and the working tree is clean. All acceptance criteria are met:

| AC | Evidence |
|----|----------|
| 1. `install --global` → XDG global + `~/.{claude,agents}/skills/<name>` | `TestInstallGlobal_WritesXDGAndAgentTierLinks` ✓ |
| 2. `install` (in-tree) → `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>` + project links | `TestInstallProject_InTreeLinks` ✓ |
| 3. `install` (convention) → XDG `projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>` + links into it | `TestInstallProject_ConventionLinks` ✓ |
| 4. `update --global` updates only global, project untouched | `TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject` ✓ |
| 5. `update` (no flag) respects active mode | `TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode` ✓ |
| 6. `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/...` green | both `ok`, exit 0 ✓ |
| 7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes | exit 0, all gates ✓ |

**What changed:**
- `cli/cmd/install.go` — `--global`/`--silent` flags; new `installLayered` core (copies plugin tree into `<root>/plugins/<name>`, links `<name>` into both agent-tier skill surfaces → `<pluginDir>/skills/<name>`, symlink with copy fallback), plus `runInstallGlobal`, `installConvention`, `resolveInstallSource`, `recordInstalledEntry`.
- `cli/internal/registry/installer.go` — exported `FetchPackageSource` to materialize a release tarball into a temp source dir.
- `cli/cmd/update.go` + `command_factory_commands.go` — `--global` flag; `performUpdateGlobal` (global-only refresh) and `refreshProjectPluginTree` (mode-aware project refresh).
- Tests: added AC1–AC5; removed the obsolete `TestInstall_GlobalFlagRemoved` (it asserted the now-restored flag was gone — this bead reinstates `--global`, but pointed at the XDG global layer, not `$HOME/.ddx`, so it stays consistent with FEAT-015's no-`$HOME/.ddx/plugins` invariant).

The in-tree no-`--global` registry path deliberately retains the proven manifest installer to avoid regressing multi-skill plugins; the new layered installer is used for the global and convention layers.
